messages.content: You are a Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) compliance auditor. Analyze the clinical encounter notes, match the correct ICD-10 diagnosis codes, and highlight billing code variances in valid JSON format only.

Encounter Notes: 58yo male presents to the ED with crushing substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm. EKG shows ST-segment elevation in leads V1-V4. Troponin markedly elevated. Cardiology confirms an acute anterior wall STEMI and the patient is taken emergently for PCI with stent placement to the LAD. Billing submitted code: I21.9.

You are a Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) compliance auditor. Analyze the clinical encounter notes, match the correct ICD-10 diagnosis codes, and highlight billing code variances in valid JSON format only.

Encounter Notes: 71yo female with a history of hypertension presents with palpitations and irregular heartbeat. EKG confirms atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response. Patient is started on rate control therapy and anticoagulation. Billing submitted code: I48.91.

You are a Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) compliance auditor. Analyze the clinical encounter notes, match the correct ICD-10 diagnosis codes, and highlight billing code variances in valid JSON format only.

You are a Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) compliance auditor. Analyze the clinical encounter notes, match the correct ICD-10 diagnosis codes, and highlight billing code variances in valid JSON format only.

You are a Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) compliance auditor. Analyze the clinical encounter notes, match the correct ICD-10 diagnosis codes, and highlight billing code variances in valid JSON format only.

Encounter Notes: A blood specimen is drawn in-office and processed using the practice's own in-house lab equipment to run a comprehensive metabolic panel. The practice separately bills a specimen handling and conveyance fee in addition to the panel. Billing submitted code(s): 99000, 80053.

You are a Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) compliance auditor specializing in CPT/HCPCS procedure coding. Analyze the clinical encounter and billing details, verify correct use of modifiers, NCCI bundling edits, and medical necessity documentation, and report compliance variances in valid JSON format only.

Encounter Notes: A blood specimen for a lipid panel is drawn in-office and packaged for transport to an outside reference laboratory for processing. The practice bills the handling/conveyance code in addition to the venipuncture; the panel itself is billed separately by the reference lab. Billing submitted code(s): 99000, 36415.

{
  "status": "APPROVED",
  "variance_found": false,
  "variance_type": "bundling",
  "submitted_codes": [
    "99000",
    "36415"
  ],
  "correct_codes": [
    "99000",
    "36415"
  ],
  "justification": "Correct - the handling/conveyance fee is appropriately billable when a specimen is transferred to an outside laboratory, separate from the venipuncture code."
}

Encounter Notes: A dermatologist performs a shave biopsy of a suspicious lesion, immediately reviews it as concerning, and proceeds within the same encounter to completely excise the same lesion. Both the biopsy and the excision of the same lesion are billed. Billing submitted code(s): 11102, 11401.

You are a Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) compliance auditor specializing in CPT/HCPCS procedure coding. Analyze the clinical encounter and billing details, verify correct use of modifiers, NCCI bundling edits, and medical necessity documentation, and report compliance variances in valid JSON format only.

Encounter Notes: A dermatologist performs a shave biopsy of a suspicious lesion on the patient's back and, in the same encounter, excises a separate, unrelated benign lesion on the patient's forearm. Both procedures are billed, with modifier 59 appended to the biopsy code to indicate a distinct anatomic site. Billing submitted code(s): 11102-59, 11401.

{
  "status": "APPROVED",
  "variance_found": false,
  "variance_type": "bundling",
  "submitted_codes": [
    "11102-59",
    "11401"
  ],
  "correct_codes": [
    "11102-59",
    "11401"
  ],
  "justification": "Correct - modifier 59 appropriately identifies the biopsy as a distinct procedural service performed at a separate anatomic site from the excision, supporting separate reimbursement."
}

You are a Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) compliance auditor specializing in CPT/HCPCS procedure coding. Analyze the clinical encounter and billing details, verify correct use of modifiers, NCCI bundling edits, and medical necessity documentation, and report compliance variances in valid JSON format only.

You are a Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) compliance auditor specializing in CPT/HCPCS procedure coding. Analyze the clinical encounter and billing details, verify correct use of modifiers, NCCI bundling edits, and medical necessity documentation, and report compliance variances in valid JSON format only.

Encounter Notes: During a diagnostic colonoscopy, the physician separately biopsies two lesions in anatomically distinct segments of the colon that are documented as clinically unrelated findings requiring separate pathology. No modifier is appended to differentiate the services from the standard bundled edit pair. Billing submitted code(s): 45380, 45385.

{
  "status": "REJECTED",
  "variance_found": true,
  "variance_type": "modifier",
  "submitted_codes": [
    "45380",
    "45385"
  ],
  "correct_codes": [
    "45380-59",
    "45385"
  ],
  "justification": "Without modifier 59 (or a more specific X-modifier) to indicate the biopsy addressed a distinct, separately documented lesion from the snare polypectomy, the payer's NCCI edit will bundle the services despite the distinct-site documentation supporting unbundling."
}

You are a Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) compliance auditor specializing in CPT/HCPCS procedure coding. Analyze the clinical encounter and billing details, verify correct use of modifiers, NCCI bundling edits, and medical necessity documentation, and report compliance variances in valid JSON format only.

Encounter Notes: Patient undergoes a chest X-ray in the morning to assess a suspected pneumothorax, then a follow-up chest X-ray of the same view is performed later the same day by the same radiologist to confirm resolution after a chest tube intervention. Both X-rays are billed with the identical CPT code and no modifier. Billing submitted code(s): 71046, 71046.

{
  "status": "REJECTED",
  "variance_found": true,
  "variance_type": "modifier",
  "submitted_codes": [
    "71046",
    "71046"
  ],
  "correct_codes": [
    "71046",
    "71046-76"
  ],
  "justification": "Repeating the same imaging study by the same physician on the same day requires modifier 76 (repeat procedure by same physician) on the second occurrence to indicate a medically necessary repeat study rather than a duplicate claim."
